title: Standardized Tests - What I think about that

transcript.text: Hello, this video is what I think about standardized tests.
I've talked a lot about standardized tests in other videos about education,
But I think I'm going to dedicate a video just specifically for it,
Because it's really important.
Education should be based...
Somebody's knowledge about stuff should be based on their scores on standardized tests.
I think that's pretty much common knowledge. I shouldn't explain why.
I think the educational system and the meritocracy is based on that more in England.
Then in America, which is a good thing. It makes England education better.
In America, your grades are basically mainly based on the whim of your teacher,
Whether or not your teacher likes you.
And that's kind of, you know, like, that's old school.
It's like, get in the king's favor type of stuff. It's not a real meritocracy.
So, there should be two criteria, or I guess three criteria,
On how good somebody is, or I guess four, mainly, but it should mainly be standardized tests.
Also, your personal website. I guess that should be the number one,
How good your personal website is.
Standardized tests should definitely be the measuring stick of how well knowledgeable you are.
And the way that they give standardized tests should be different than the way it is now.
The way it is now, you take standardized tests when you graduate high school,
And then you only have one chance to take them.
Standardized tests should be a test that you can take whenever you want,
As many times as you want, whenever you want.
So, if you take a test, and you don't like the grade that you got,
Because the score that you got, because it's not high enough to get into a certain school,
Or to get a certain job, or you want to get a better,

And it shouldn't be standardized tests with the technology.

There's no way you can cheat.
